title: An Immense World
author: Ed Yong
subline: Every animal lives in its own sensory bubble.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- A grid of 6 animal silhouettes in their umwelt (sense bubble). Each animal is surrounded by a circle that grows/contracts to show their sensory reach — bat = sound (wide), star-nosed mole = touch (medium), dog = smell (wide low), mantis shrimp = color (tight), snake = heat (wide), human = small. -->
  <g transform="translate(800 1100)" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" text-anchor="middle">
    <!-- Cell 1: BAT (echolocation, large upward ring) -->
    <g transform="translate(-400 -240)">
      <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="180" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.4"/>
      <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="120" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.6"/>
      <text x="0" y="-6" font-size="60" font-weight="500" fill="#cccccc">B</text>
      <text x="0" y="240" font-size="13" font-weight="500" fill="#cccccc" letter-spacing="0.18em">BAT · ECHO</text>
    </g>
    <!-- Cell 2: STAR-NOSED MOLE (touch) -->
    <g transform="translate(0 -240)">
      <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="100" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.4"/>
      <text x="0" y="-6" font-size="60" font-weight="500" fill="#cccccc">M</text>
      <text x="0" y="240" font-size="13" font-weight="500" fill="#cccccc" letter-spacing="0.18em">MOLE · TOUCH</text>
    </g>
    <!-- Cell 3: DOG (smell) -->
    <g transform="translate(400 -240)">
      <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="160" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.4"/>
      <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="100" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.6"/>
      <text x="0" y="-6" font-size="60" font-weight="500" fill="#cccccc">D</text>
      <text x="0" y="240" font-size="13" font-weight="500" fill="#cccccc" letter-spacing="0.18em">DOG · SCENT</text>
    </g>
    <!-- Cell 4: MANTIS SHRIMP (color, RED because they see 16 colors vs human 3) -->
    <g transform="translate(-400 120)">
      <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="100" fill="none" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.6"/>
      <text x="0" y="-6" font-size="60" font-weight="500" fill="#fc1c46">S</text>
      <text x="0" y="240" font-size="13" font-weight="500" fill="#fc1c46" letter-spacing="0.18em">SHRIMP · 16 COLORS</text>
    </g>
    <!-- Cell 5: SNAKE (heat) -->
    <g transform="translate(0 120)">
      <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="160" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.4"/>
      <text x="0" y="-6" font-size="60" font-weight="500" fill="#cccccc">N</text>
      <text x="0" y="240" font-size="13" font-weight="500" fill="#cccccc" letter-spacing="0.18em">SNAKE · HEAT</text>
    </g>
    <!-- Cell 6: HUMAN (small) -->
    <g transform="translate(400 120)">
      <circle cx="0" cy="0" r="60" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.6"/>
      <text x="0" y="-6" font-size="60" font-weight="500" fill="#cccccc">H</text>
      <text x="0" y="240" font-size="13" font-weight="500" fill="#cccccc" letter-spacing="0.18em">HUMAN · 3</text>
    </g>
  </g>
</svg>
rationale: A 2x3 grid of six animals, each surrounded by concentric circles representing the size of their umwelt (perceptual bubble). The mantis shrimp cell is RED because the book marvels at its 16-color vision vs the human 3. 280px whisper title at the bottom.
